name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Alignment (round-down) must never produce a value larger than the original address

name: aligned_input_changes
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_input_changes(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning an already-aligned value must be a no-op; changing it would corrupt valid base addresses

name: result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The whole point of alignment is that the result is divisible by the alignment size; violating this breaks page-boundary invariants

name: excessive_rounding
code:
proof fn phi_4_excessive_rounding(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
{
}
reason: Rounding down by b or more means skipping an entire alignment block, losing a valid mapping region

name: not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_5_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Alignment must be idempotent; a double-align changing the result would mean walk result vbase addresses are unstable
